captured_from: GHA run 24213258406 — first clean capture under portable RUSTFLAGS (-C target-cpu=x86-64-v2, see bench.yml comment). All 20 workloads captured in a single run on Azure-pool ubuntu-24.04, post-Mission-D10 and post-Mission-F (LTO + codegen-units=1 + panic=abort).
note: Per-workload absolute gate is +/-7% default (storage + legacy PowQL) and +/-10% for noisy write/sort workloads (see compare.rs::threshold_for). The massive regression shown against the OLD btree_lookup=1832 and seq_scan_filter=13738549 numbers (btree -85.68%, seq_scan_filter -51.70%) is genuine improvement from Mission D (read fast paths) + Mission F (LTO), NOT noise — the pre-Mission-D baseline was captured before cross-crate inlining and the compiled-predicate scan walker landed. btree_lookup dropped disproportionately (7x) vs powql_point (2.87x), which strains the powql_point_over_btree_lookup thesis ratio — see thesis-ratios.json for the relax commentary.
